mnemonics.title: Fix vs Variable Partitioning
mnemonics.content: FIXED: FIVE - Fixed has Internal fragmentation, Variable has External fragmentation. MEMORIZE: FIXED PARTITIONS = INTERNAL PROBLEMS, VARIABLE = EXTERNAL PROBLEMS

mnemonics.title: Fragmentation Types
mnemonics.content: INTERNAL: Inside the box (partition). EXTERNAL: Outside, scattered around (exist outside allocated blocks). REMEMBER: Internal = INside, External = EXternal/EXited from partition

acronyms.usage: Hardware component that translates logical addresses to physical addresses using base and limit registers in contiguous allocation.
Contains process information including base and limit register values for memory protection.
Replacement strategy; not specifically for contiguous allocation but used in conjunction for memory management.

visualTips.visualization: Draw boxes representing partitions. Internal: A large box with a smaller item inside and shaded waste space. External: Multiple small empty boxes (holes) scattered between occupied boxes.
Imagine desk with papers (processes) and empty spaces scattered. Compaction = pushing all papers together to create one large empty space.

Think of a hotel (memory) with room numbers (physical addresses). Base register = floor number where your room starts, Limit = how many rooms you can use. Room you want = offset. Actual room = floor + room number.

analogies.analogy: Like fixed-size parking slots in a lot. A small car (small process) in a large slot wastes space (internal fragmentation). A bus (large process) won't fit in small slots.
Like having scattered small coins but needing a larger bill. You have enough total money but it's not in usable form. Compaction is like exchanging all coins for one large bill.
Like trying on shoes. Best Fit finds the smallest shoe that fits your foot comfortably, minimizing extra space. First Fit is like grabbing the first shoe that fits without trying others.
Base register is like the starting address of your house on a street. Your house number (logical address) + street starting number (base) = actual house number (physical).
